entities.name: GOVERNING THROUGH THE COMMUNITY
entities.description: A policy approach used by Australian governments to interpret neoliberal policy and manage rural communities through intermediaries.

entities.name: NEOLIBERAL POLICY
entities.description: The policy framework embraced by Australian governments since the 1990s.

entities.name: AUSTRALIA
entities.description: The country where the case study on farmer organizations is conducted.

entities.name: FARMER ORGANIZATIONS
entities.description: Groups in rural Australia that serve the community and act as representatives to the state.

entities.name: LANDCARE GROUPS
entities.description: A specific type of farmer organization that uses paperwork to articulate interaction mechanisms.

entities.name: GROWER GROUPS
entities.description: A specific type of farmer organization that uses data to articulate interaction mechanisms.

entities.name: FARMER COOPERATIVES
entities.description: A specific type of farmer organization that uses price to articulate interaction mechanisms.

entities.name: GOVERNMENT AGENCIES
entities.description: State entities that interact with and regulate community representatives and members.

entities.name: COMMUNITY REPRESENTATIVES
entities.description: Intermediaries who make the community visible and governable to the state.

entities.name: COMMUNITY MEMBERS
entities.description: Individuals within the community who may experience tensions with their representatives.

entities.name: CASE STUDY
entities.description: The research method used to analyze farmer organizations in two Australian states.

entities.name: PAPERWORK
entities.description: A theme used by Landcare groups to articulate how their interactions are regulated.

entities.name: DATA
entities.description: A theme used by grower groups to articulate how their interactions are regulated.

entities.name: PRICE
entities.description: A theme used by farmer cooperatives to articulate how their interactions are regulated.
